NCT05096221.name: EMBARK
NCT05096221.group: Ambulatory DMD boys 4-7 yrs, delandistrogene moxeparvovec single IV vs placebo (52-wk NSAA score change MD; did not meet primary)

allOutcomes.title: NSAA total score change from baseline at week 52 (primary; MD vs placebo)
Time to rise from floor (Gowers manoeuvre) at week 52 (seconds; MD vs placebo; favours less time)
10-meter walk/run velocity at week 52 (m/s; MD vs placebo)

NCT05096221.snippet: Source: Mendell JR et al. NEJM 2024 (EMBARK; primary not met but functional secondaries favoured gene therapy).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: MALE; min age 4 Years; max age 7 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Is ambulatory and from 4 to under 8 years of age at time of randomization.~* Definitive diagnosis of DMD based on documented clinical findings and prior genetic testing.~* Ability to cooperate with motor assessment testing.~* Stable daily dose of oral corticosteroids for at least 12 weeks prior to Screening, and the dose is expected to remain constant throughout the study (except for modifications to accommodate changes in weight).~* rAAVrh74 antibody titers are not elevat...

title: RapidMeta Neuromuscular | Delandistrogene Moxeparvovec Gene Therapy for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy v1.0
h1: Delandistrogene Moxeparvovec Gene Therapy \u2014 Patient Summary
